sentence_reviews.text: Encoder-only transformers use bidirectional self-attention to contextualize an entire input for representation learning, classification, retrieval, and token-level prediction.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Devlin et al. 2018 BERT describes deep bidirectional Transformer pretraining, contextual representations, and fine-tuning for sentence- and token-level tasks; retrieval is not a primary BERT claim.
sentence_reviews.notes: Bidirectional contextual representation, classification, and token-level prediction are supported; retrieval broadens beyond the cited BERT evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: They separate contextual understanding from autoregressive generation and remain central to embedding and discriminative NLP systems.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: BERT contrasts bidirectional pretraining with left-to-right language models and demonstrates discriminative NLP fine-tuning.
sentence_reviews.notes: The non-autoregressive/discriminative distinction is supported; 'remain central' is a broad present-tense claim.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: BERT is pretrained with masked language modeling using a bidirectional Transformer encoder.
BERT evidence supports bidirectional encoders, but the packet does not cite a retrieval/bi-encoder source.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible application, but not directly evidenced by the cited source.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: BERT reports fine-tuning for token-level tasks such as question answering and named entity recognition-style tagging.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported by BERT token-level task use.

sentence_reviews.text: They do not natively generate long outputs autoregressively and usually need task-specific heads or a separate decoder.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: BERT is an encoder/pretraining model for representation and fine-tuning, not a left-to-right decoder; task-specific output layers are used for fine-tuning tasks.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation follows from BERT's encoder-only bidirectional design; long-output generation is not directly discussed.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoder-only bidirectional transformers extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: BERT is explicitly a deep bidirectional Transformer model; Vaswani et al. defines the Transformer architecture.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoder-only bidirectional transformers is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Masked prediction objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: BERT pretraining uses masked language modeling.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for BERT-style encoder-only transformers.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoder-only bidirectional transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Causal, masked & structured masking.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: BERT masks input tokens for masked language model pretraining.
sentence_reviews.notes: Masking is directly supported; causal masking is not used but the atlas label includes masked mechanisms.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoder-only bidirectional transformers consumes, emits, or organizes information using Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: BERT is a language representation model using token/position embeddings and contextual representations.

edge_reviews.notes: BERT is explicitly a Transformer encoder model; core specializes edge is supported.
Masked language modeling is central to BERT-style encoder pretraining.
Input masking is directly supported; common strength is appropriate.
Token, segment, position, and contextual embeddings support the represents_with edge.
